description: The CAPABILITIES const declared at module scope in clangd-core/extractor.ts. Originally I targeted clangdCoreExtractor itself (line 85, also a module-level const), but that one is declared `const X = defineExtractor({...})` then `export default X` separately — and the ts-core extractor currently does NOT extract non-exported top-level consts even if they're later default-exported. CAPABILITIES is also declared as `const CAPABILITIES: Capability[] = [...]` (line 43) and IS extracted because... well, this fixture documents whichever path actually populates the graph. Backend gap: the default-export-after-const pattern should be picked up too.

contract.notes: Two real backend gaps documented in this fixture's description: (1) non-exported top-level consts that are later default-exported are not extracted, (2) `defineExtractor({...})`-style factory results have no synthetic plugin-registration edge today. The intelgraph pack would close gap 2.
